tool.name: RGI main
tool.description: identify antimicrobial resistance genes using CARD
tool.command: mkdir -p staging && rgi load @CMD_CARD_LOAD_ARGS@ @CMD_WILDCARD_LOAD_ARGS@ --local && rgi main -i '$input_sequence' -o staging/output -t '$input_options.input_type' -a '$alignment_tool' -n \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4} -d '$data_type' #if str($input_options.input_type) == "contig" -g '$input_options.orf_finder' #end if $hit_inclusion.include_loose $hit_inclusion.include_nudge $hit_inclusion.low_quality --local --clean

param.name: input_sequence
param.label: Input sequences
param.help: FASTA or gzip-compressed FASTA file containing contigs or protein sequences.

param.name: input_type
param.label: Input type
param.help: Nucleotide contigs require ORF prediction before alignment. Protein sequences are aligned directly, skipping gene prediction.

param.name: orf_finder
param.label: ORF finding tool
param.help: PRODIGAL is the standard prokaryotic gene predictor. PYRODIGAL is a faster Python reimplementation that produces near-identical predictions.

param.name: alignment_tool
param.label: Alignment tool
param.help: DIAMOND is 20-100x faster than BLAST with minimal sensitivity loss for typical AMR databases. Use BLAST for small inputs or when maximum sensitivity is required.

param.name: data_type
param.label: Sequence data type
param.help: Genomic origin of the input sequences. Used to annotate results and does not change alignment behaviour. Select the most specific category that applies.

param.label: Include loose hits
param.help: Report hits that partially satisfy CARD model thresholds (Loose hits). Increases sensitivity but raises the false-positive rate. By default only Perfect and Strict hits are reported.

param.label: Include nudged hits
param.help: Promote qualifying Loose hits to Strict classification based on genomic context (e.g. co-location with other AMR determinants). Only has an effect when loose hits are also enabled.

param.label: Low quality / short contigs mode
param.help: Relax ORF prediction thresholds to recover partial or truncated genes from fragmented assemblies. Recommended for low-coverage or short-read assemblies.

tool.help:
**RGI main — Resistance Gene Identifier**
Identifies antimicrobial resistance (AMR) genes and mutations in genomic or protein sequences by aligning them against the Comprehensive Antibiotic Resistance Database (CARD).
-----
**Inputs**
- **Input sequences** — FASTA file of nucleotide contigs or translated protein sequences. gzip-compressed FASTA is accepted. - **CARD database** — Select a pre-installed CARD release (provided by your Galaxy administrator) or supply files from your history. Download the latest release from https://card.mcmaster.ca/download. - **Annotation model type** — Controls which annotation FASTA is loaded. *Homolog models only* is sufficient for standard AMR gene identification. *All model types* loads the extended annotation database. - **WildCARD database** — Optional. Provide to enable detection of predicted AMR gene variants not yet in canonical CARD (see WildCARD section below).
-----
**Input type**
- *Contig* (default) — nucleotide sequences. RGI calls PRODIGAL or PYRODIGAL to predict open reading frames before alignment. ORF finder and parallelism options appear when this type is selected. - *Protein* — translated amino acid sequences. ORF prediction is skipped and sequences are aligned directly against CARD protein models.
-----
**Alignment tool**
- *DIAMOND* (default) — 20–100× faster than BLAST with minimal sensitivity loss. Recommended for assemblies with hundreds or thousands of contigs. - *BLAST* — more sensitive; use for small inputs or when every potential hit matters.
-----
**Sequence data type**
Labels results with the genomic origin of the input. Does not change alignment behaviour. Select the most specific option that applies:
- *Not specified (NA)* — use when the source is unknown or mixed. - *Whole-genome shotgun* — short-read WGS assemblies. - *Plasmid* — sequences known to be plasmid-derived. - *Chromosome* — complete or draft chromosomal assemblies.
-----
**WildCARD variants**
When a WildCARD database is loaded above, RGI main automatically searches in silico predicted allelic variants in addition to canonical CARD models. WildCARD captures resistance gene diversity not yet represented in canonical CARD, improving detection of novel or divergent variants. No additional flag is required — simply provide the WildCARD database files to enable it.
-----
**Hit inclusion options**
By default RGI reports only **Perfect** and **Strict** hits, which fully satisfy curated CARD model thresholds.
- *Include loose hits* — also reports Loose hits that partially satisfy thresholds. Increases sensitivity but raises the false-positive rate. Use for exploratory analysis or when screening for distant homologs. - *Include nudged hits* — promotes qualifying Loose hits to Strict based on genomic context (e.g. co-location with other AMR determinants). Only has an effect when loose hits are also enabled. - *Low quality / short contigs mode* — relaxes ORF prediction thresholds to recover partial or truncated genes from fragmented assemblies. Recommended for low-coverage or highly fragmented short-read assemblies.
-----
**Outputs**
- **AMR results (tabular)** — tab-delimited table of all detected AMR determinants, including ORF coordinates, CARD ARO accession, best-hit identity and coverage, drug class, resistance mechanism, and Perfect/Strict/Loose classification. - **AMR results (JSON)** — full output in JSON format, compatible with the CARD Resistome Gene Identifier web viewer and downstream parsing tools.
-----
**References**
Alcock et al. (2020). CARD 2020: antibiotic resistome surveillance with the Comprehensive Antibiotic Resistance Database. *Nucleic Acids Research*, 48(D1), D517–D525. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkz935
RGI GitHub: https://github.com/arpcard/rgi
